opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My mouth is full of ulcers and it's so painful I haven't been able to drink a drop of water all day. I'm starting to feel a bit dizzy.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I can't keep up with fluids and I live alone — I'm worried I'll get dehydrated overnight.

symptom_narrative.onset: started about 48 hours ago, much worse today
symptom_narrative.character: multiple painful mouth ulcers, raw and stinging
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: 8 out of 10 when I try to swallow
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: inside my cheeks and the roof of my mouth
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: cold water helps briefly but it stings
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: a bit dizzy when I stand, no fever, no rash, no eye or genital problems
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get mouth ulcers on and off but never this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can't eat or drink properly, feeling weak

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: recurrent mouth ulcers over the years

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: a couple of tablets, barely helping

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone, no one checking on him today

communication_style.typical_message_style: types in short bursts due to discomfort

access_barriers.transport: can get a taxi/rideshare if needed

objective_data_offered.value: several small shallow ulcers with red rims inside the cheeks and on the soft palate, lips look a bit dry
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: phone-camera view of the mouth; cannot assess posterior pharynx or true hydration status
